summary.business_type: Content/utility site — free employment-law calculators (YMYL-adjacent, pre-monetization), heavy programmatic SEO (~225 location pages)
top_findings: CRITICAL structural flag: sitemap contains ~225 location-style pages (US state x4 variants, CA provinces, AU states) — 4.5x the quality-gate hard-stop threshold of 50. HOWEVER the content-quality spot-check independently PASSED: 51-95% unique content per pair, genuinely state-specific statutory data, not templated filler. The scale itself (not the content) is the risk — Google may read it as index bloat given 46% of the sitemap shares a placeholder lastmod date and several page types (pto-payout variants, situations pages) are only linked via the sitemap, not internal navigation.

CRITICAL: the new /uk/redundancy pillar page is entirely one-directional — none of its 16 linked spokes (calculators, guides, FAQs) link back to it. Fails the mandatory bidirectional pillar-link pattern and wastes the topical-authority value the pillar was built to create.

CRITICAL (SXO): two FAQ pages (/faq/what-is-overtime-law-us, /faq/what-is-tupe-transfer) are single 285-315 word paragraphs competing against SERPs dominated by long-form state-by-state tables and government/law-firm guides — and don't even link to the site's own better-suited calculator/state pages that would help them rank.

Real Google API data now available for the first time (Tier 1 credentials configured this session) — closed the prior audit's biggest data gap. Real Lighthouse/PSI confirms excellent lab CWV (LCP 0.5-1.7s, CLS <=0.012) across all templates. CrUX has no field data yet (expected — insufficient traffic volume, not a defect). New gap surfaced: the GSC service account has no access to the mypayrights.com property, blocking real indexation/search-performance data — a 2-minute fix (add as Full user in Search Console).

Real visual/rendered bug found (not visible from source review): floating trust badges overlap and hide the 'Basic pay' and 'Notice pay' values in the homepage hero result card at >=1024px (desktop/tablet only — not present on mobile).
Structural E-E-A-T gap carried forward and reconfirmed: no named, credentialed legal reviewer anywhere on the site for YMYL employment-law content across UK/US/CA/AU jurisdictions.
GEO/AI-citation readiness improved to 93/100 — llms.txt is now live and high quality (new since last audit), closing the prior audit's only AI-Search-Readiness gap.

Content-cluster internal linking score rose from 34 to 58/100 — the redundancy-pay compare/FAQ cannibalization risk flagged in June turned out to be a non-issue on inspection (well-differentiated by intent), but the new pillar page's one-directional linking (finding above) is an unresolved regression risk.

Backlink profile is effectively zero (site too new for Common Crawl inclusion) — expected for site age, not a defect; flagged with a prioritized digital-PR/citation-outreach plan given the site's real citation-worthy assets (50-state calculators).
quick_wins: Add claude-seo@demoin-1057.iam.gserviceaccount.com as a Full user on the mypayrights.com Search Console property (~2 minutes) — unlocks real indexation status and search-performance data for every future audit.
Fix the desktop/tablet (>=1024px) trust-badge overlay that hides the homepage hero's Basic pay / Notice pay values — CSS z-index/positioning fix, no content change needed.
Add reciprocal links from /uk/redundancy's 16 spoke pages back to the pillar — mechanical internal-linking fix using the existing link matrix in findings/cluster.md.
Standardize 'My Pay Rights' branding — replace 14+ hardcoded 'MyPayRights' (no space) instances with the shared SITE.name constant already used elsewhere.
Add the `image` property to Article/BlogPosting JSON-LD using the opengraph-image route that already exists per-page — a one-line schema fix, no new asset needed.

Link /faq/what-is-overtime-law-us and /faq/what-is-tupe-transfer to their corresponding calculator/state pages — immediate internal-linking fix, no new content required for the link itself (though both pages also need depth added, see action plan).

categories.name: Technical SEO
what_works: robots.txt permissive for all crawlers including AI bots (GPTBot, anthropic-ai, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, PerplexityBot, CCBot), correctly declares sitemap
Sitemap grew 358 -> 411 URLs with zero duplicates; local build audit (audit-indexability.mjs / score-technical-seo.mjs) is 100/100 across all 411 routes
Security headers (CSP, HSTS preload, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy) fully consistent across every page type sampled, including the 404 page
Genuine 404s (not soft-404s) with correct no-store caching; clean single-hop redirects (www->apex, http->https, trailing-slash->canonical)
Zero JS-rendering dependency (fully SSR/SSG, is_spa:false everywhere); render-blocking resources improved from 1 stylesheet (Jul 8) to zero (CSS now fully inlined)
Complete, per-page-unique meta tags (description, full OG set, Twitter Cards); broad JSON-LD presence across all page types; hreflang correctly scoped to locale hubs only

findings.title: IndexNow protocol not implemented
findings.description: No IndexNow key file served, zero repo references to indexnow. Site adds new URLs frequently (53 in 6 days) without pinging Bing/Yandex/Naver on publish.
findings.recommendation: Add a generated key file to public/ and a post-deploy step that POSTs new/changed URLs to api.indexnow.org/indexnow.

findings.title: Sitemap lastmod is a placeholder date on ~46% of URLs
findings.description: 188/411 URLs (US state pages) carry a generic 2025-01-01 lastmod, unchanged since Jul 8 audit. Risks Google discounting lastmod as a signal site-wide.
findings.recommendation: Wire US state page generator to a real content-verified date, or omit lastmod where no real date is tracked.

findings.title: Case-sensitive routes 404 instead of normalizing
findings.description: Mixed-case URLs (e.g. /Redundancy-Pay-Calculator) 404 instead of 301-redirecting to canonical casing on this static-export/Cloudflare Pages host.
findings.recommendation: Add a Cloudflare _redirects rule or functions/ middleware to lowercase and 301 mismatched-case paths.

findings.title: Mobile tap-target sizing only confirmed at source level
findings.description: Viewport meta correct and unlocked on all sampled pages; actual rendered sizing is covered by the visual audit (see Visual/UX section).
findings.recommendation: None from this category — see Visual findings.

findings.title: Strong lab CWV source signals, cross-reference against real field data
findings.description: Zero render-blocking CSS, async scripts, no raster images/@font-face on sampled pages.
findings.recommendation: None; maintain current build pattern as pages are added.

categories.name: Content Quality
what_works: Location-page quality gate: PASS — 51-95% unique content across every sampled state/province pair, genuinely differentiated statutory data (deadlines, DOL/agency links), not find-replaced boilerplate

A previously-documented HARD-STOP duplication defect (16-18% unique on federal-minimum-wage-tier states) was fixed across three prior iterations and confirmed still holding at the current, larger scale (411 vs 262 URLs) — quality improved, did not regress
Real, checkable source citations on every sampled page (named publisher, state labor agency link, federal DOL reference)
Location-page word counts comfortably clear the 500-600 floor for final-paycheck, minimum-wage, and pto-payout variants

findings.title: Two of five sampled state hub pages fall below the word-count floor
findings.description: Kansas (409 words) and Mississippi (406 words) sit below the 500-600 word floor for location pages, likely correlated with the 'federal-minimum, no-PTO-statute' state cluster.
findings.recommendation: Add one genuinely state-specific content block (enforcement agency detail, recent legislative change) to hub pages in the shared-tier cluster.

findings.title: No visible per-page 'last verified' date stamp on US state pages
findings.description: Unlike UK calculators ('Rates verified 6 April 2026'), no dated freshness badge was found in extracted text on US state pages — may be a genuine gap or an extraction artifact, not confirmed against raw HTML.
findings.recommendation: Confirm via raw HTML inspection; add a dated freshness badge to US/CA/AU templates matching the UK pattern.

findings.title: No named, credentialed legal reviewer
findings.description: Sole named author is a software engineer; no solicitor/attorney review on any page across UK/US/CA/AU YMYL content. Carried forward from the 27 June audit, likely still open.
findings.recommendation: Engage a qualified employment-law professional per jurisdiction to review calculator logic and legal copy; display name, credential, review date. Highest-leverage E-E-A-T improvement available.

findings.title: Cross-state comparison tables do a lot of the 'uniqueness' lifting
findings.description: The 30-40 row 'Cross-state deadline benchmark' table is a re-sorted view of the same shared 51-state dataset on every page — legitimate but worth monitoring as the narrative-to-tabular ratio shifts.
findings.recommendation: Continue periodic re-sampling of the most-similar state pairs, tracking narrative-to-tabular content ratio.

findings.title: Other E-E-A-T items carried forward, not re-verified this session
findings.description: Methodology/editorial-policy depth, privacy policy, compare/FAQ duplication were documented in the Jul 8 and Jun 27 audits but not re-fetched this session per audit scope.
findings.recommendation: Re-verify on next pass, particularly the named-reviewer gap and freshness-badge question.

categories.name: On-Page SEO
what_works: Every sampled route has a self-referential canonical, robots: index,follow, exactly one H1, and passing title/description length (100/100 on local build audit across all 411 routes)
Meta descriptions, full Open Graph set, and Twitter Cards verified real and per-page-unique (not placeholders) on multiple page types
Breadcrumbs present via BreadcrumbList schema on all deep page types

findings.title: Two FAQ pages critically thin relative to their competing SERP
findings.description: /faq/what-is-overtime-law-us (285 words) and /faq/what-is-tupe-transfer (315 words) are single-paragraph answers competing against SERPs dominated by long-form state-by-state tables and government/law-firm guides.
findings.recommendation: Expand both pages to genuine long-form depth matching what ranks, and add contextual links to the site's own calculator/state pages that address the same query.

findings.title: Minimal in-content cross-linking to the site's own better-suited tools
findings.description: /tupe-wizard, /take-home-overtime-calculator, and /compare/* pages exist but aren't surfaced contextually from the FAQ pages that most need them.
findings.recommendation: Add contextual in-body links from FAQ/guide pages to the relevant calculator or comparison tool.

findings.title: Homepage hero result card: floating trust badges obscure key values at desktop widths
findings.description: Confirmed via rendered screenshot at 1024/1280/1366/1920px: trust badges overlap and hide the 'Basic pay' and 'Notice pay' fields in the sample result card. Not present on mobile.
findings.recommendation: Fix z-index/positioning of the trust-badge row relative to the result card at >=1024px breakpoints.

findings.title: Mobile cookie-consent banner overlaps hero search widget submit button
findings.description: On mobile, the consent banner obscures the primary CTA above the fold until dismissed.
findings.recommendation: Reposition consent banner or reserve space so it doesn't overlap the primary interactive element.

findings.title: Several mobile tap targets under the 44x44px guideline
findings.description: Hamburger menu (36x36), homepage quick-link pills (35px tall), consent-banner buttons (40px tall), footer privacy link (16px tall).
findings.recommendation: Increase touch-target sizing to meet the 44x44px accessibility guideline across these elements.

categories.name: Schema / Structured Data
what_works: Broad JSON-LD coverage across every page type: WebSite/Organization (home), BreadcrumbList/WebApplication/FAQPage (calculators), Article/FAQPage (blog, compare, state pages)
FAQPage/QAPage correctly retained post-Google's May 2026 FAQ rich-result retirement — not stripped, since it retains real AI/LLM citation value
Local build validation (score-schema-seo.mjs) is 100/100 on parse validity and required-field checks

findings.title: Article/BlogPosting JSON-LD missing required `image` property
findings.description: Blog posts, FAQ-as-Article, and compare pages omit `image`, a Google-required Article property — traces to the site's deliberate zero-raster-image build.
findings.recommendation: Reference the existing per-route opengraph-image endpoint (already live on calculator pages) in the Article schema's image field.

findings.title: Inconsistent publisher entity name across templates
findings.description: 'My Pay Rights' (via shared SITE.name constant) vs hardcoded 'MyPayRights' (no space) in 14+ templates (/faq/*, /compare/*, /guides/*, two /us/states/[state]/* templates). Weakens entity-resolution consistency for Google/AI.
findings.recommendation: Replace hardcoded 'MyPayRights' strings with the shared SITE.name constant across all affected templates.

findings.title: Article publisher objects omit `logo`
findings.description: Google-recommended `logo` property is missing from publisher objects; the asset (logo-mark.svg) already exists and is used correctly elsewhere.
findings.recommendation: Add the existing logo asset reference to the publisher object in Article schema.

findings.title: FAQPage schema no longer earns Google SERP rich results (informational)
findings.description: Per the May 7, 2026 full retirement, FAQPage schema has no remaining Google SERP benefit sitewide.
findings.recommendation: Keep as-is — no removal, no QAPage conversion needed (editorial, not user-submitted Q&A); retained for AI/LLM citation value only.

findings.title: Stale 2025 year reference embedded in a live schema-text block
findings.description: A California state-page FAQPage answer contains a hardcoded '2025' reference despite dateModified being 2026-07-09 — AI engines may cite the schema text verbatim.
findings.recommendation: Fix the hardcoded year reference in the affected FAQ answer text.

categories.name: Performance (CWV)
what_works: Real Google PageSpeed Insights v5 (Lighthouse) data collected for the first time this audit (Tier 1 API credentials now configured) — replaces prior audit's fully-inferred 78/100
Homepage, calculator page, and state page all score 100/100 Lighthouse Performance on mobile and desktop, LCP 0.5-1.7s, CLS <=0.012 — comfortably 'Good' band
Zero render-blocking resources; consistent with the site's zero-image, static-export architecture

findings.title: No CrUX field data available yet
findings.description: Both CrUX API and CrUX History API return 'insufficient Chrome traffic volume for eligibility' for the origin and all sampled URLs. Expected for a site at this traffic stage, not a defect.
findings.recommendation: Re-check monthly as organic traffic grows; treat lab data as the best available proxy until then.

findings.title: Homepage mobile LCP borderline (2.56s on one run)
findings.description: Right at the 2.5s Good/Needs-Improvement boundary while calculator (1.68s) and blog (1.69s) are comfortably under; desktop is fine everywhere.
findings.recommendation: Identify and preload the homepage's actual LCP element; re-test with margin.

findings.title: Accessibility regressions on calculator and state page templates
findings.description: Real Lighthouse audits fail color-contrast, heading-order, and link-in-text-block checks on /redundancy-pay-calculator and /us/states/california (scores 89-91 vs 100 on homepage); state page additionally fails a label/accessible-name mismatch on 'Switch country'.
findings.recommendation: Fix contrast ratios, heading order, and link distinguishability on the calculator and state page templates (template-level fix propagates to all instances).

findings.title: Missing CSP Trusted Types and COOP headers
findings.description: Lighthouse flags no CSP Trusted Types directive and no Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header as High-severity security diagnostics; current CSP also allows 'unsafe-inline' for script-src.
findings.recommendation: Add COOP: same-origin and a Trusted-Types-enforcing CSP at the Cloudflare/edge config level.

findings.title: GSC service account lacks access to the mypayrights.com property
findings.description: The authenticated service account has access to a different property (easyphoto.in) but not mypayrights.com — blocking real indexation status, URL Inspection, and search-performance (clicks/impressions/CTR/position) data.
findings.recommendation: Add claude-seo@demoin-1057.iam.gserviceaccount.com as a Full user on the mypayrights.com Search Console property (~2 minutes), then re-run.

categories.name: AI Search Readiness
what_works: llms.txt is now live at https://mypayrights.com/llms.txt (new since Jul 8 audit) — unusually high quality: full page inventory, named sources per jurisdiction, dated statutory figures, explicit AI Citation Guidelines and Permissions sections. Closes the prior audit's only AI-Search-Readiness gap.
robots.txt explicitly allows GPTBot, anthropic-ai, ClaudeBot, Google-Extended, PerplexityBot, CCBot
All sampled pages fully server-rendered/static with FAQPage/Article/BreadcrumbList/WebApplication JSON-LD, named authorship, and source-adjacent citations present in raw HTML an AI crawler receives
FAQPage/QAPage schema correctly retained post-May-2026 Google FAQ retirement for its AI-citation value

findings.title: No independently-verified brand-mention signals
findings.description: A live web/social search for 'mypayrights.com' mentions (Wikipedia/Reddit/YouTube/LinkedIn) was not executed this session, leaving the strongest AI-citation predictor (YouTube mentions, ~0.737 correlation) unmeasured.
findings.recommendation: Run a dedicated brand-mention sweep; consider a YouTube explainer video given the outsized correlation vs. domain-rating correlation (~0.266).

findings.title: Calculator pages have thin H2 sub-structure
findings.description: /redundancy-pay-calculator has strong explanatory prose but isn't broken into question-phrased H2/H3 units the way FAQ pages are, limiting independently-citable passages.
findings.recommendation: Restructure calculator explanatory text into question-phrased H2/H3 sections.

findings.title: No RSL 1.0 licensing manifest alongside llms.txt
findings.description: The prose Permissions section in llms.txt already substantively covers citation permissions; RSL is an emerging, not-yet-critical standard.
findings.recommendation: Optional — add if/when RSL adoption becomes more standard.

findings.title: Multi-modal content signal remains weak
findings.description: Zero raster images, no video, no charts on any sampled page, capping the Multi-Modal AI-citation dimension regardless of text citability strength elsewhere. Unchanged from prior audit.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding at least one chart/diagram type per major content template.

categories.name: Images
what_works: Zero raster images site-wide (fully SVG/CSS/system-font) — eliminates image-weight and unsized-image CLS risk entirely, reconfirmed incidentally by both the technical and performance audits this pass

findings.title: Zero raster images forgoes image-search visibility
findings.description: Carried forward from the Jul 8 audit — an intentional architectural trade-off, not re-independently re-audited this pass (no dedicated image agent run).
findings.recommendation: If image-search traffic becomes a priority, consider adding illustrative diagrams/charts for key calculator/guide pages (also benefits AI Search Readiness multi-modal signal).

supplementary_analyses.name: Content Topic Clusters & Internal Linking
supplementary_analyses.note: Not part of the 7-category weighted health score; reported separately per skill methodology. Up from 34/100 on 27 June 2026.

top_findings.title: Pillar-to-spoke linking is one-directional
top_findings.description: /uk/redundancy fans out to 6 calculators, 4 guides, and 6 FAQs, but none of those spokes link back to the pillar — verified by direct link extraction on all 6 redundancy-topic page types.
top_findings.recommendation: Add reciprocal links from every spoke page back to /uk/redundancy using the link matrix in findings/cluster.md.

top_findings.title: Guide vs. blog cannibalization unresolved
top_findings.description: /guides/uk-redundancy-pay and /blog/uk-redundancy-pay-guide-2026 have near-identical titles/H1s/structure at comparable depth with no cross-link. Flagged in the June 27 audit, still unfixed.
top_findings.recommendation: Cross-link the two pages and differentiate their angle/depth, or merge.

top_findings.title: Compare and FAQ pages have no path to the pillar or specific guide
top_findings.description: They link generically to /guides or /faq hubs but never to /uk/redundancy, /guides/uk-redundancy-pay, or /situations/made-redundant-uk.
top_findings.recommendation: Add specific contextual links, not just hub-level links.

top_findings.title: Blog is the weakest-linked content format sitewide
top_findings.description: Only links to 2 calculators, nothing else.
top_findings.recommendation: Add contextual links from blog posts to relevant calculators, guides, and FAQs.

top_findings.title: Redundancy-page cannibalization concern was a non-issue on inspection
top_findings.description: Each /compare/ page pairs redundancy against a genuinely distinct concept; FAQs each target a distinct single question. No real overlap found.
top_findings.recommendation: None needed — false alarm from the prior audit's assumption, now verified via real link/content extraction.

supplementary_analyses.name: Search Experience Optimization (SXO)
supplementary_analyses.note: Not part of the 7-category weighted health score; reported separately per skill methodology.

top_findings.title: Overtime FAQ ignores the site's own competing assets
top_findings.description: /faq/what-is-overtime-law-us is a 285-word single paragraph while the SERP is dominated by state-by-state comparison tables; doesn't link to the site's own take-home-overtime-calculator or /us/states/* daily-overtime data.
top_findings.recommendation: Expand with state-by-state data and link to the site's own calculator/state pages.

top_findings.title: TUPE FAQ critically under-depth for a definitional YMYL query
top_findings.description: /faq/what-is-tupe-transfer is 315 words vs long-form guides from Acas, CIPD, GOV.UK, and Wikipedia covering transfer types, ETO reasons, and consultation duties.
top_findings.recommendation: Expand to match competing depth on transfer types, ETO reasons, and consultation duties.

top_findings.title: E-E-A-T authority gap across all YMYL legal content
top_findings.description: All sampled pages cite a non-credentialed founder/editorial-team byline while direct SERP competitors are government agencies or named law firms with attorney credentials.
top_findings.recommendation: Same as the Content Quality category's named-reviewer finding — highest-leverage fix, addresses both categories at once.

top_findings.title: Zero images/visual aids on every page sampled
top_findings.description: No formula diagrams, flowcharts, or timelines anywhere in the 4-page sample.
top_findings.recommendation: Add at least one visual aid type per major template (overlaps with the Images/AI-Search findings).

top_findings.title: Page-type alignment is a real strength, not a mismatch
top_findings.description: Calculator (Tool) and state pages (Hybrid content+tool) are correctly aligned with what Google rewards for those query types — the gap is concentrated in the two thin FAQ pages and site-wide authority signals, not a fundamental architecture problem.
top_findings.recommendation: None — reinforces continuing the current page-type strategy for calculators and state pages.

supplementary_analyses.name: Backlink Profile
supplementary_analyses.note: Not part of the 7-category weighted health score. Scored as directional-floor/insufficient-data per the skill's own validation gate — site predates the latest Common Crawl snapshot, this is a timing artifact of site age, not a low-authority signal.

top_findings.title: Zero referring domains detected (expected for site age)
top_findings.description: mypayrights.com does not appear in the latest Common Crawl web graph at all (in_crawl: false) — the site's earliest content post-dates that crawl's collection window.
top_findings.recommendation: Not a defect; re-check as the site accumulates crawl history.

top_findings.title: Prioritized link-acquisition plan
top_findings.description: Site has real citation-worthy assets: full 50-state + DC final-paycheck/minimum-wage/PTO coverage, UK/Canada/Australia jurisdiction coverage, ~30 free calculators.
top_findings.recommendation: 1) .gov/.edu/legal-aid citation outreach for state pages. 2) HARO-style journalist outreach tied to layoff news cycles. 3) HR/employment-law blog outreach for guides. 4) Comparison pages as citation bait. 5) Free-tool directory submissions.

top_findings.title: Add Moz + Bing Webmaster API keys before next audit cycle
top_findings.description: Currently Tier 0 (Common Crawl only) — no DA/PA or spam-score data available.
top_findings.recommendation: Register free API keys so the next audit pass has real backlink signal once initial links exist.

phases.name: Phase 1: Critical Fixes
items: Add claude-seo service account as a Full user on the mypayrights.com Search Console property (2 min) — unblocks real indexation/search-performance monitoring going forward
Fix desktop/tablet (>=1024px) trust-badge overlay hiding Basic pay / Notice pay values in the homepage hero result card
Add reciprocal links from all 16 /uk/redundancy spoke pages back to the pillar
Expand /faq/what-is-overtime-law-us and /faq/what-is-tupe-transfer to genuine long-form depth and link them to the site's own calculator/state pages

Explicitly review and justify (or reduce) the ~225 location-page footprint against the quality-gate hard-stop threshold — content quality independently passed, but document the decision given the scale

phases.name: Phase 2: High-Impact Improvements
items: Engage a qualified employment-law professional per jurisdiction (UK/US/CA/AU) to review calculator logic and legal copy; display name, credential, review date
Fix inconsistent 'MyPayRights' vs 'My Pay Rights' entity naming across 14+ templates by using the shared SITE.name constant
Add `image` and `logo` properties to Article/BlogPosting JSON-LD using the existing opengraph-image endpoint and logo-mark.svg asset
Cross-link /guides/uk-redundancy-pay and /blog/uk-redundancy-pay-guide-2026 or differentiate/merge them to resolve cannibalization risk
Fix color-contrast, heading-order, and link-in-text-block accessibility issues on the calculator and state page templates (template-level fix propagates broadly)
Add COOP header and a Trusted-Types-enforcing CSP at the Cloudflare edge config level
Fix mobile consent-banner overlap of the hero search widget's submit button and increase under-sized tap targets to 44x44px

phases.name: Phase 3: Content & Authority
items: Add a dated 'last verified' freshness badge to US/CA/AU state pages matching the existing UK calculator pattern
Add IndexNow protocol support (key file + post-deploy ping to Bing/Yandex/Naver)
Add a state-specific content block (enforcement agency detail, recent legislative change) to hub pages below the 500-600 word floor (Kansas, Mississippi, Wyoming)
Add contextual links from blog posts to relevant calculators/guides/FAQs (currently the weakest-linked content format)
Begin prioritized link-acquisition outreach: .gov/legal-aid citations for state pages, HARO-style journalist outreach, HR/employment-law blog outreach
Run a dedicated brand-mention sweep (YouTube, Reddit, Wikipedia, LinkedIn) — highest-correlation AI-citation signal currently unmeasured

phases.name: Phase 4: Monitoring & Iteration
items: Re-check CrUX field data monthly as organic traffic grows — currently no eligible data, expected for site's traffic stage
Fix case-sensitive route 404s with a lowercase-and-301 redirect rule
Fix the ~46% of sitemap URLs sharing a placeholder 2025-01-01 lastmod date
Register free Moz + Bing Webmaster API keys to unlock real backlink signal on the next audit
Periodically re-sample the most-similar state-page pairs to confirm the location-page content-uniqueness gate continues to pass as new pages are added
